definition.en: It is a DCAT distribution that is defined or is to be generated in a managed manner by means of a transformation or a validation.
A planned set of operations intended by one or more agents aiming at converting an input data distribution into an output data distribution conforming to a given set of rules.

It is an activity that carries out a transformation over a period of time actually converting the data of the transformation input source distribution into data then included in a output distribution. It inherits the prov:startedAtTime and prov:endedAtTime properties of the class prov:Activity in order to specify when the activity started and ended.
Any information returned by the TransformationExecution activity, e.g; return code or message when the execution was processed without error.
The text data type is a combination of a string and a language identifier.

anyURI represents an Internationalized Resource Identifier Reference (IRI).  An anyURI value can be absolute or relative, and may have an optional fragment identifier (i.e., it may be an IRI Reference).
DateTime values may be viewed as objects with integer-valued year, month, day, hour and minute properties, a decimal-valued second property, and a boolean timezoned property.
nonNegativeInteger is derived from integer by setting the value of minInclusive to be 0.
The string datatype represents character strings in XML.
An idea or notion; a unit of thought.
NOTE
An entity is a physical, digital, conceptual, or other kind of thing with some fixed aspects; entities may be real or imaginary.

A specific representation of a dataset. A dataset might be available in multiple serializations that may differ in various ways, including natural language, media-type or format, schematic organization, temporal and spatial resolution, level of detail or profiles (which might specify any or all of the above).
An unambiguous reference to the resource within a given context.
A name given to the resource.

usage.en: This property can be repeated for parallel language versions of the title.

definition.en: The status of the Standard.

usage.en: The values to be used for this property are the URIs of the concepts in the status code list.

definition.en: End is when an activity is deemed to have been ended by an entity, known as trigger. The activity no longer exists after its end. Any usage, generation, or invalidation involving an activity precedes the activity's end. An end may refer to a trigger entity that terminated the activity, or to an activity, known as ender that generated the trigger.
Generation is the completion of production of a new entity by an activity. This entity did not exist before generation and becomes available for usage after this generation..

Start is when an activity is deemed to have been started by an entity, known as trigger. The activity did not exist before its start. Any usage, generation, or invalidation involving an activity follows the activity's start. A start may refer to a trigger entity that set off the activity, or to an activity, known as starter, that generated the trigger.
Association that defines the target into which the input distribution is converted to.
Association to the planned transformation which occurred.
Association that defines a distribution of the data source to be transformed
